With the wacli skill available, the agent immediately understood the correct command syntax (--to, --message, --query, --chat, --json flags), executed the full workflow in 2 steps (via the skill task), performed all required operations (doctor health check, per-recipient chats list resolution, per-recipient messages search for conflicts, three correctly-targeted send text calls), and produced a complete delivery report. Without the skill, the agent discovered wacli binary but could not determine the correct argument syntax, spent 14 steps trying various incorrect invocations, failed to send any messages (all returned 'unknown recipient'), and failed to create the report file. The skill provided critical knowledge of the wacli API contract that made the difference between complete success and total failure.
